history.content: I have ADHD and find it hard to focus on long paragraphs. Can you make the text shorter and use bullet points? Also, I get distracted by animations, so please keep the interface simple.
Thank you for sharing. I'll simplify the text and use clear headings and bullet points. I'll also avoid any moving elements.
I have dyslexia. Words seem to blur together. Can you use a special font like OpenDyslexic and increase line spacing? Also, cream background helps.
I'll apply OpenDyslexic font, increase line spacing, and set a cream background. Let me know if you need further adjustments.
I'm on the autism spectrum. I need clear structure and no metaphors. Please use literal language and avoid surprises like pop-ups.
Understood. I'll use straightforward language, organize content clearly, and ensure no unexpected elements appear.
I have low vision. Can you increase contrast and make text larger? Also, buttons should be big and easy to click.
I'll increase text size, enhance contrast, and enlarge buttons for better visibility.
I have both ADHD and dyslexia. Long texts are overwhelming, and I also struggle with blurry words. Can you use bullet points and the OpenDyslexic font?
I'll break content into bullet points, use OpenDyslexic font, and ensure good spacing. I'll keep it simple and distraction-free.
I have anxiety. Please avoid any flashing or moving elements. I prefer calm colors and clear instructions.
I'll use a calm color palette, remove all animations, and provide clear step-by-step instructions.
I have trouble processing information quickly. Can you present content in small chunks and use simple language?
I'll break content into small, digestible chunks and use simple, direct language throughout.
I'm colorblind. Don't rely on color alone to convey information. Use patterns or icons instead.
I'll avoid color-based indicators and use icons and patterns to convey information clearly.
I have epilepsy. No flashing content at all. Please ensure nothing blinks or flickers.
I'll remove all flashing and flickering elements and ensure the interface is static and safe.
I have a traumatic brain injury. I need extra time to read and understand. Please avoid auto-advancing content.
I'll ensure nothing auto-advances and give you full control over pacing. Take your time.
I have ADHD and get easily distracted by cluttered layouts. Can you keep it minimal with lots of white space?
I'll use a clean, minimal design with plenty of white space and remove any unnecessary elements.
I have dyslexia and also struggle with numbers. Can you use clear fonts and maybe include visual aids?
I'll use OpenDyslexic font and include simple visual aids to support numerical information.
I'm on the spectrum and need routines. Please keep the layout consistent across pages.
I'll maintain consistent layout and navigation throughout so you always know where things are.
I have low vision and use screen magnification. Make sure the layout doesn't break when zoomed in.
I'll ensure the layout is responsive and remains usable when zoomed in.
I have both autism and anxiety. I need predictability and calm colors. Please avoid any sudden changes.
I'll use a calm, predictable interface with consistent behavior and no sudden changes.
I have ADHD and also some vision issues. Can you use high contrast and keep paragraphs short?
I'll use high contrast colors and break text into short, scannable paragraphs.
I have dyslexia and prefer listening over reading. Is there an option to have text read aloud?
I'll include text-to-speech compatibility and ensure screen reader support.
I have epilepsy and also use a screen reader. Please ensure all images have alt text and no flashing.
I'll add alt text to all images and remove any flashing elements for safety.
I have a processing disorder. Please use simple sentences and avoid jargon.
I'll use simple language, avoid technical terms, and explain any necessary concepts clearly.
I have ADHD and also tend to skim. Can you use bold headings and highlight key points?
I'll use clear headings and highlight key information so it's easy to spot while skimming.
I have dyslexia and also experience migraines. Please use low-contrast colors, not bright white.
I'll use a softer color palette with reduced contrast to minimize strain.
I'm on the spectrum and get overwhelmed by too much information. Can you show only what's essential?
I'll strip away non-essential information and focus only on what's needed for the task.
I have low vision and use a screen reader. Please ensure all buttons and links are properly labeled.
I'll ensure all interactive elements have clear, descriptive labels for screen reader compatibility.
I have ADHD and also memory issues. Can you include reminders and keep instructions visible?
I'll keep key instructions visible and include gentle reminders where helpful.
I have dyslexia and also struggle with left-right orientation. Please avoid directional language like 'see left'.
I'll avoid directional references and use clear labels instead.
I have anxiety and need confirmation before actions. Please add 'are you sure?' prompts.
I'll include confirmation dialogs for important actions to reduce anxiety about mistakes.
I have both ADHD and dyslexia. I need both structure and special fonts. Can you combine both?
I'll use OpenDyslexic font with clear structure, headings, and bullet points.
I have photosensitive epilepsy. Absolutely no animations or transitions.
I'll disable all animations and keep the interface completely static.
I'm colorblind and also have low vision. Use patterns and large text.
I'll combine large text with patterns and icons to convey information without relying on color.
I have autism and need extra time. Please don't time out my sessions.
I'll disable session timeouts so you can work at your own pace.
I have ADHD and also dyscalculia. Can you use visual aids for numbers and keep text short?
I'll use visual representations for numerical data and keep text concise and scannable.
I have dyslexia and prefer sans-serif fonts. Can you use Arial or Verdana?
I'll use a clean sans-serif font like Arial for better readability.
I have anxiety and get stressed by cluttered interfaces. Please keep it very simple.
I'll create a simple, uncluttered interface with only essential elements.
I have low vision and also use voice control. Make sure buttons have clear names for voice commands.
I'll ensure all buttons have clear, unique names for voice control compatibility.
I have both autism and dyslexia. I need clear structure AND special fonts. Can you do both?
I'll combine OpenDyslexic font with clear hierarchical structure and consistent layout.
I have ADHD and need frequent breaks. Can you save my progress automatically?
I'll implement auto-save so you can take breaks without losing progress.
I have epilepsy and also use a screen reader. Please ensure both safety and accessibility.
I'll remove all flashing content and ensure full screen reader compatibility.
I have a memory disorder. Please keep instructions visible and repeat important information.
I'll keep key instructions on screen and repeat important information where needed.
I have ADHD and also sensitivity to sound. Please avoid auto-playing media.
I'll disable all auto-playing media and give you control over any audio/video.
I have dyslexia and also struggle with small text. Can you use larger font and special font?
I'll use OpenDyslexic font at a larger size for better readability.
I'm on the spectrum and need clear expectations. Please explain what will happen before I click.
I'll provide clear descriptions of what each button does before interaction.
I have low vision and also colorblindness. Use patterns and high contrast together.
I'll combine high contrast with patterns to ensure information is accessible.
I have anxiety and ADHD. Keep it simple, calm, and structured.
I'll create a calm, structured environment with simple navigation and no surprises.
I have dyslexia and also use text-to-speech. Ensure proper punctuation for natural speech.
I'll use proper punctuation and grammar to support natural text-to-speech reading.
I have both epilepsy and low vision. No flashing, and text must be large.
I'll use large text with no flashing elements, ensuring both safety and readability.
I have ADHD and need interactive elements to stay engaged. Can you add simple interactions?
I'll add simple, predictable interactive elements to maintain engagement without distraction.
I have autism and sensory sensitivities. Please use muted colors and avoid bright lights.
I'll use a muted, soothing color palette and avoid bright, harsh colors.
I have dyslexia and also need help with navigation. Can you add a clear path forward?
I'll provide clear navigation cues and a visible path through the content.
I have low vision and use magnification. Ensure text doesn't get cut off when zoomed.
I'll ensure all text remains visible and doesn't overflow when magnified.
I have ADHD and also anxiety about deadlines. Can you show progress without pressure?
I'll show gentle progress indicators without time pressure or deadlines.
I have dyslexia and also migraines. Use dyslexia-friendly font and reduce blue light.
I'll use OpenDyslexic font with warm, reduced-blue color tones.
I'm on the spectrum and need literal language. No idioms or sarcasm, please.
I'll use only literal language and avoid all figurative expressions.
I have epilepsy and also use keyboard navigation. Ensure everything is keyboard accessible.
I'll ensure full keyboard accessibility and remove any flashing content.
I have ADHD and need variety to stay focused. Can you use different formats (lists, images)?
I'll mix formats with short text, lists, and relevant images to maintain interest.
I have dyslexia and also struggle with columns. Please use single-column layout.
I'll use a simple single-column layout for easier reading flow.
I have anxiety and need reassurance. Can you include encouraging messages?
I'll include gentle, encouraging messages throughout the experience.
I have low vision and also ADHD. Use large text AND keep it concise.
I'll use large, readable text while keeping content brief and focused.
I have both dyslexia and epilepsy. Special font, no flashing, cream background.
I'll combine OpenDyslexic font, cream background, and remove all flashing content.
I have autism and need warning before changes. Please alert me before anything updates.
I'll provide clear alerts before any content updates or changes.
I have ADHD and also use a screen reader. Keep it simple and properly labeled.
I'll keep the interface simple and ensure all elements are properly labeled for screen readers.
I have dyslexia and also need high contrast. Can you do both special font and good contrast?
I'll use OpenDyslexic font with high contrast colors for maximum readability.
I have anxiety and epilepsy. Calm colors and no flashing, please.
I'll use a calm color palette and ensure no flashing elements are present.
I have low vision and also use voice control. Large text and clear button names.
I'll combine large text with clearly named buttons for voice control compatibility.
I have ADHD and dyslexia. Short paragraphs, bullet points, and special font.
I'll use OpenDyslexic font with short paragraphs and bullet points for easy scanning.
I'm on the spectrum and need predictable navigation. Same menu on every page.
I'll keep navigation identical across all pages for consistency.
I have epilepsy and also dyslexia. No flashing, special font, cream background.
I'll combine all safety and accessibility features: no flashing, OpenDyslexic font, cream background.
I have anxiety and ADHD. Keep it simple but also engaging enough to hold focus.
I'll create a clean interface with just enough engagement to maintain focus without causing anxiety.
I have dyslexia and low vision. Large OpenDyslexic font and high contrast.
I'll use enlarged OpenDyslexic font with high contrast colors.
I have autism and need extra processing time. No time limits anywhere.
I'll remove all time limits and let you work at your own pace.
I have ADHD and also get overwhelmed by too many choices. Limit options on each screen.
I'll present only a few options at a time to prevent choice overload.
I have epilepsy and also use screen magnification. No flashing, zoom-friendly layout.
I'll ensure no flashing content and a layout that remains usable when zoomed.
I have dyslexia and anxiety. Special font, calm colors, clear structure.
I'll combine OpenDyslexic font with calm colors and clear organizational structure.
I have low vision and also need simple language. Large text and plain English.
I'll use large text with simple, straightforward language.
I have ADHD and autism. Need both structure and engagement. Clear but not boring.
I'll create structured content with predictable but engaging elements.
I have dyslexia and also need help with focus. Special font and remove distractions.
I'll use OpenDyslexic font and remove all non-essential visual elements.
I have epilepsy and anxiety. No flashing, calm colors, predictable interface.
I'll create a calm, predictable interface with no flashing and soothing colors.
I have ADHD and also memory issues. Keep instructions visible and repeat key points.
I'll keep instructions on screen and repeat important information throughout.
I have low vision and dyslexia. Large OpenDyslexic font, high contrast.
I'll use enlarged OpenDyslexic font with maximum contrast.
I'm on the spectrum and need clear boundaries. Please separate different sections visually.
I'll use clear visual separation between different sections and content types.
I have epilepsy and also use keyboard navigation. No flashing, full keyboard support.
I'll ensure full keyboard accessibility and remove all flashing elements.
I have ADHD and also need visual breaks. Can you add some white space between sections?
I'll add generous white space between sections for visual breathing room.
I have dyslexia and also struggle with crowded text. Please increase letter spacing too.
I'll increase both line spacing and letter spacing for better readability.
I have anxiety and need to know what to expect. Please explain each step before I start.
I'll provide clear overviews of each step before you begin.
I have low vision and ADHD. Large text AND short, scannable content.
I'll combine large, readable text with short, scannable content blocks.
I have both autism and epilepsy. Predictable, no flashing, calm colors.
I'll create a predictable, calm interface with no flashing and consistent behavior.
I have dyslexia and also need help with navigation. Clear 'next' and 'back' buttons always visible.
I'll keep navigation buttons clearly visible and consistently placed.
I have ADHD and also sensory sensitivities. Keep it simple and avoid bright colors.
I'll use a simple design with muted, gentle colors.
I have epilepsy and also need high contrast. No flashing, but good contrast is essential.
I'll provide high contrast while ensuring no flashing elements are present.
I have anxiety and dyslexia. Calm colors, special font, clear structure.
I'll combine OpenDyslexic font with calm colors and clear organization.
I have low vision and also use screen reader. Large text and proper labels for everything.
I'll ensure large text size and comprehensive labeling for screen reader compatibility.
I have ADHD and need tasks broken down. Please show one thing at a time.
I'll present information one piece at a time to prevent overwhelm.
I have dyslexia and also need help with forms. Clear labels and examples.
I'll provide clear labels and examples for all form fields.
I'm on the spectrum and need explicit instructions. Don't assume I understand implied steps.
I'll provide explicit, step-by-step instructions with no implied steps.
I have epilepsy and also use voice control. No flashing, clear button names for voice.
I'll ensure no flashing content and clear button names for voice control.
I have ADHD and also need positive reinforcement. Can you add some encouragement?
I'll include positive feedback and encouragement throughout.
I have dyslexia and also need help with numbers. Use digits instead of words for numbers.
I'll use digits (e.g., '5' instead of 'five') for better number recognition.
I have anxiety and need control. Please let me choose when to move to next section.
I'll give you full control over pacing with manual progression only.
I have low vision and epilepsy. Large text, no flashing, high contrast.
I'll combine large text, high contrast, and no flashing for safety and readability.
I have ADHD and autism. Need both structure and engagement. Predictable but not boring.
I'll create predictable patterns with subtle engagement elements.
I have dyslexia, ADHD, and anxiety. Can you combine all: special font, short text, calm colors, clear structure?
I'll create a comprehensive accessible interface with OpenDyslexic font, concise content, calm colors, and clear organization.
